events.title: 43 Students Forcibly Disappeared in Iguala
events.detail: Students from the Ayotzinapa Rural Teachers' College were attacked by municipal police in Iguala, Guerrero. 43 students were taken and have not been seen since. 6 people were killed and 25 injured in the attacks.

events.title: Government Announces 'Historical Truth'
events.detail: Attorney General Jesús Murillo Karam presents the 'historical truth': students were killed by Guerreros Unidos cartel and incinerated at Cocula garbage dump. This version is later debunked by independent investigators.

events.title: GIEI Debunks 'Historical Truth'
events.detail: The Interdisciplinary Group of Independent Experts (GIEI), appointed by the IACHR, publishes its first report concluding that the government's version is scientifically impossible. The Cocula dump could not have reached temperatures needed to incinerate 43 bodies.

events.title: Truth Commission: 'Crime of State'
events.detail: The Ayotzinapa Truth and Access to Justice Commission declares the disappearance a 'crime of state' involving coordination between government officials, security forces, and organized crime.
